name: Magic Circle
description: A rotating arcane circle on the ground/air: concentric glowing rings, runic glyphs, a slow rotation with counter-rotating layers, an inner glow, and a summon/activation pulse. Built with Three.js ring geometry + additive blending + a texture or shader for runes.

requiredElements.name: concentric-rings
requiredElements.description: At least 2-3 concentric glowing rings of varying radius forming the circle structure.

requiredElements.name: runic-glyphs
requiredElements.description: Runic/symbolic glyphs placed around a ring (texture-mapped or instanced) for arcane feel.

requiredElements.name: counter-rotation
requiredElements.description: Layers rotate at different speeds and directions (counter-rotating) for a living mechanism.

requiredElements.name: inner-glow
requiredElements.description: An inner radial glow/aura at the circle center.

requiredElements.name: activation-pulse
requiredElements.description: A pulse/flash on activation (summon moment) that expands outward and settles.

optionalElements.name: rising-particles
optionalElements.description: Spark particles rising from the ring edges.

optionalElements.name: ground-decal
optionalElements.description: A faint ground decal beneath the circle for grounding.

qualityRules.description: Rings read clearly as concentric circles with even spacing.
Glyphs are visible and evenly distributed, not cluttered.
Inner glow gives the circle depth, not flat.
Rotation is smooth and continuous, counter-rotating layers visible.
Activation pulse has clear anticipation and settle.
Rings use additive blending and depthWrite=false for proper glow overlap.
Ring segment count bounded; rotation via quaternion not rebuild.

antiPatterns.description: One flat static ring with no rotation or glyphs looks like a decal, not magic.
All layers rotating the same speed/direction looks like one object, not a mechanism.
Leaving depthWrite=true on additive rings causes z-fighting/sorting artifacts.

improvementSuggestions.description: Add rising spark particles from the ring for life.
Add a faint ground decal to ground the circle in the scene.

exampleOutputs.label: Counter-rotating rings
exampleOutputs.snippet:
<group>
  <mesh rotation={[-Math.PI/2,0,0]}>
    <ringGeometry args={[0.8,1,64]} />
    <meshBasicMaterial color="#7fdfff" blending={THREE.AdditiveBlending} transparent depthWrite={false} />
  </mesh>
  <mesh ref={inner} rotation={[-Math.PI/2,0,0]}>
    <ringGeometry args={[0.5,0.55,48]} />
    <meshBasicMaterial color="#bfefff" blending={THREE.AdditiveBlending} transparent depthWrite={false} />
  </mesh>
</group>
